text:
Based on my web search results, I have found the exact paper that matches the cited reference with a minor author name formatting difference.

The paper exists at: 
Li, D., Brüschweiler, R. PPM_One: a static protein structure based chemical shift predictor. J Biomol NMR 62, 403–409 (2015)


The cited reference uses "Dawei Li" while the published paper shows the author as "D." or "Da-Wei Li" depending on the source (some versions show "Dawei Li" and others show "Da-Wei Li" with a hyphen). This is a minor formatting variation of the same author name. The title, venue, year, and co-author all match perfectly.

EXPLANATION: The exact paper was found in the Journal of Biomolecular NMR with the cited title, authors (with minor name formatting variation), and year. The reference is genuine.
LINK: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10858-015-9958-z
FOUND_TITLE: PPM_One: a static protein structure based chemical shift predictor
FOUND_AUTHORS: Dawei Li, Rafael Brüschweiler
FOUND_YEAR: 2015
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
